entry.title: Streamlining Compliance with SAP Localization for Qatar Withholding Tax and Payment Retentions.
entry.content:
Compliance with local regulations is crucial for seamless operations in today's global business environment. For businesses operating in Qatar, understanding and adhering to the local requirements for supplier payment retentions and withholding tax (WHT) is essential. SAP’s localization solution for Qatar offers a robust framework to ensure compliance with these regulations, as outlined in Circular No. 2 from 2011.

Understanding the Regulatory Landscape

Qatar’s tax regulations mandate that certain final contract amounts must be retained from payments made to temporary branches. This retention is either 3% of the contract value (excluding the val

ue of supply and work carried out abroad) or the final contractual payment, whichever is higher. The retained amount is held until the temporary branch provides a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the General Tax Authority (GTA).

SAP Localization Solution for Qatar

SAP’s localization solution for Qatar was developed to help businesses streamline compliance with these regulations efficiently. The solution integrates seamlessly with SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, providing automated processes for managing supplier payment retentions and withholding tax.

The solution integrates the specific requirements for supplier payment retentions and withholding tax into the SAP system, ensuring that all necessary checks and validations are performed automatically.

Key Features

    1. Automated Validation:
      • The SAP localization solution automatically checks the company code, tax card, and commercial registration status, ensuring compliance before processing payments.

        To activate the enhanced solution for retention-based validations in SAP S/4HANA using the customizing “Activate Enhanced Localization for Withholding Tax and Retention for Qatar” (view V_FIAPQAC_AP_MIG). Learn more about the activation of enhanced solution at this link.
        To activate retention-based validations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, use “Activate Validations for Withholding Tax and Payment Retention Liability” (SSCUI ID: 105883).
        The activation blocks excess payments to suppliers.
        Maintain unique payment block reasons with “Check Payment Block Reason” (SSCUI ID: 101040).
        For automatic payments, map these reasons using “Specify Payment Blocks for Validation Categories” (SSCUI ID: 104350).

         

    2. Retention Logic:
      • The solution includes the latest QA retention logic, ensuring that payments are withheld appropriately based on the supplier's compliance status.
      • The solution automatically calculates the retention amount based on the contract value and the specific criteria outlined in the regulations.
      • Before processing payments, the solution checks for the presence of a valid NOC from the GTA. If the NOC is not available, the payment is retained.

  1. Withholding Tax Calculation:
    • The solution ensures that withholding tax is applied correctly based on the supplier’s residency status, registration status, contract duration, and the necessary certificates as required by law.

  2. Error Handling and Warnings:
    • The system raises appropriate warnings and errors if any compliance issues are detected with the supplier’s tax card or commercial registration, preventing non-compliant payments from being processed.

Ensuring Smooth Operations

By leveraging SAP’s localization solution, businesses can ensure compliance with Qatar’s regulatory requirements, minimizing the risk of penalties and operational disruptions. The solution's automated processes and comprehensive checks streamline the management of supplier payments and withholding tax, allowing businesses to focus on their core operations.

Supplier Payment Retentions and Withholding Tax

Supplier payment retentions and withholding tax are standard practices to ensure that suppliers fulfill their contractual obligations. In Qatar, the process involves several key steps and checks:

  1. Payment Initiation: The process begins with initiating a payment in the SAP system.

  2. Company Code Validation: The system checks if the payment is associated with a Qatar company code.
    • If not, the payment proceeds without additional checks.
    • If yes, the system proceeds to the next step.

  3. Retention Logic Activation: The system verifies if the new retention logic is active.
    • If not, the old retention solution is applied.
    • If yes, the system continues with the new logic.
  4. QFC Certificate Validation: The system checks for a valid QFC (Qatar Financial Centre) certificate.
    • If valid, the process continues.
    • If not, the old retention solution is applied.

  5. Residency, Tax Card Checks, and Commercial Registration: The system verifies the supplier's residency status and the availability of a tax card. Payments cannot be made to suppliers with expired commercial registrations or a tax card that was not presented.
    • Use the country-specific tab in the Maintain Business Partner app master to manage the Supplier's Tax Card and Residency status. Learn more about Qatar's Tax Card at this link.
    • Use the country-specific tab in the Maintain Business Partner app master to manage the Supplier's Certificates and residency status. Learn more about the Supplier Certificate at this link.
Retention Scenarios for non-resident suppliers:

A non-resident supplier has a non-permanent branch linked to a specific period or project at least one year

  • Retain all payments under the contract until a tax card is presented. 
  • Retain the final payment or 3% of the contract value (whichever is higher) until a valid NOC from the GTA is presented.

A non-resident supplier has a permanent branch registered in the Commercial Register.

  • Retain the final payment under the contract until a tax card is presented.

Retention Scenarios for Resident Suppliers:

A resident supplier does not have a tax card

  • Retain the final payment under the contract until a tax card is presented.

 

Withholding Tax (WHT):

Withholding tax is crucial to Qatar's tax system, particularly for non-resident suppliers. The key points include:

  1. Liability: Suppliers must ensure their tax cards and valid commercial registrations to avoid payment disruptions.
      • The Withholding tax applies to payments made to non-resident suppliers who are not registered in the Commercial Register or are registered for short-term activity contracts for less than a year.
      • The withholding tax is also applicable if the commercial registration of a non-resident supplier has expired.
      • The automatic determination of Withholding Tax for services is based on Material Group Classification as local, foreign, or exempt. For local services, it uses the configuration step Specify Service Origin and Withholding Tax Codes for Material Groups. 

        To activate the enhanced solution for retention-based validations in SAP S/4HANA using the customizing “Specift Service Origin and Withholding Tax Codes for Material Groups” (view FIWTQAV_MGC). Learn more about the activation of enhanced solution at this link.
        To activate retention-based validations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, use “Specift Service Origin and Withholding Tax Codes for Material Groups” (SSCUI ID: 104295).
        Withholding tax only applies to local services. When multiple codes exist in the supplier master data, a single tax code is selected for purchase order-based and supplier invoices.

        The supplier is subject to a couple of withholding taxes related to two services.


        Only one Withholding Tax code has been captured based on the Material Group Classification assigned



  2. Statutory Reports and Supplier Certificates: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance enables you to process and monitor the statutory reports for the use of your businesses with operations in Qatar. It comprises functions designed for laws and business practices. As a result, your business stays compliant with local legal obligations in Qatar for the following statutory reports:
    • Withholding Tax Declaration report.
    • The Withholding Tax Certificate is a correspondence form that generates all withholding tax certificates for foreign suppliers based on withholding tax transactions from the Withholding Tax Declaration report.

Conclusion

Compliance with local regulations is a critical aspect of doing business in Qatar. SAP’s localization solution for Qatar offers a comprehensive framework to efficiently manage supplier payment retentions and withholding tax. By automating key processes and ensuring rigorous compliance checks, the solution helps businesses easily navigate the complexities of Qatar’s regulatory landscape.

Useful Links

  • Learn more about the enhanced solution for Qatar Withholding Tax and Payment Retention at this link.
  • Learn more about the withholding tax statutory reports available for Qatar at this link.
  • Learn more about the old solution in SAP S/4HANA at this link.

entry.title: Enhancing the Standard XML File for Electronic Documents using the BAdI EDOC_ADAPTOR_CLOUD
entry.content:
In this blog post, we’ll explore how to enhance the standard XML file generated for an electronic document using the method SET_OUTPUT_DATA of the Business Add-In (BAdI) EDOC_ADAPTOR_CLOUD. This enhancement to the XML generated by SAP is particularly useful for modifying XML tags and filling optional XML tags with the values such as customer material number or delivery number.

We'll use the Norway eInvoice process as a reference example.

Why Enhance the XML File?

Currently, most country/region processes in the eDocument Cockpit (EDOC_COCKPIT) or the Manage Electronic Documents app only fill the mandatory XML tags. By enhancing the XML file, you can include additional information that may be relevant for your business processes, such as the customer material number in the billing document.

Prerequisites

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Developer Extensibility
  • Proxy structures must be released for Developer Extensibility by the country

Implementation Steps in ABAP Development Tools (ADT)

  1. In your ABAP project, go to the enhancement spot ES_EDOCUMENT_CLOUD (path: package GLO-EDO > Enhancements > Enhancement Spots).
  2. Right-click ES_EDOCUMENT_CLOUD and select New BAdI Enhancement Implementation.
  3. Enter a package, name, and description.
  4. Choose Add BAdI Implementation and select for EDOC_ADAPTOR_CLOUD.
  5. (Optional) Create a BAdI filter for Country and/or Generic Filter.
  6. Choose Implementing Class to create a new implementation class. The new implementation class automatically implements the interfaces "if_badi_interfaces" and "if_edoc_adaptor_cloud".
  7. After generating the class, open the interface method and press F3 to view its parameters. To display the documentation, press F2.
  8. Activate your BAdI implementation.

Example Implementation

In the following example we'll fill the XML tag <BuyersItemIdentification> with the customer material number for the electronic document created for the billing document. To read the customer material data for billing document, you first identify the sales document behind the billing document. Use “i_billingdocumentitem” and “i_salesdocumentitem” to read the respective data. If the customer material is empty for the respective sales document, read it from “I_CustomerMaterial_2”. Then, fill the XML tag <BuyersItemIdentification>, which is under the tag <Invoiceline>-<Item>-<BuyersItemIdentification>.

Note:

  • The CDS views that you use in your implementation must be released with C1 contract for developer extensibility. 
  • Use '@' in the select statements if you get an error when you copy the following sample code in your ADT editor.

 

 

 

CLASS zcl_edoc_adaptor_cloud DEFINITION

  PUBLIC

  FINAL

  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.

    INTERFACES if_badi_interface .

    INTERFACES if_edoc_adaptor_cloud .

  PROTECTED SECTION.

  PRIVATE SECTION.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_badi_edoc_adaptor_cloud IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD if_edoc_adaptor_cloud~set_output_data.

    DATA: ls_source_data TYPE                    
               if_edoc_cloud_datatypes=>mty_source_data.
    DATA: ls_proxy_structure  TYPE edo_ubl_standard_business_doc2.
    DATA: ls_inv_line TYPE edo_ubl_invoice_line.
    DATA: lt_billing_item TYPE TABLE OF I_BillingDocumentItem.
    DATA: lt_sales_document_item TYPE TABLE OF i_salesdocumentitem.
    DATA: lt_Customer_material TYPE TABLE OF I_CustomerMaterial_2.
    TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_vbeln,
            salesdocument       TYPE vbeln,
           END OF ty_vbeln.
    DATA: lt_sales_document TYPE TABLE OF ty_vbeln.

     ls_source_data = is_source_data.
     ls_proxy_structure = cs_output_data.

    CASE iv_source_type ."Check Source Type
*    When it’s a billing document and eDocument Type belongs to Norway  
      WHEN  'SD_INVOICE' AND iv_edoc_type = 'NO_INV'.  

*       Read billing document item  detail  
        SELECT * FROM i_billingdocumentitem WITH PRIVILEGED ACCESS 
          WHERE billingdocument = ls_source_data-billing_header-vbeln
          INTO TABLE lt_billing_item.

*       Collect Sales document number
        LOOP AT lt_billing_item INTO DATA(ls_billing_item).  
          DATA(lv_sales_document) = ls_billing_item-salesdocument.
          APPEND lv_sales_document TO lt_sales_document.
        ENDLOOP.

*       Read sales document item details
        SELECT * FROM i_salesdocumentitem WITH PRIVILEGED ACCESS  
        FOR ALL ENTRIES IN lt_sales_document
        WHERE salesdocument = lt_sales_document-salesdocument
        INTO TABLE lt_sales_document_item.

*       Read customer material detail for respective sales org, distribution 
*       channel, Material and sold to party information
        SELECT * FROM I_CustomerMaterial_2 WITH PRIVILEGED ACCESS 
          FOR ALL ENTRIES IN lt_sales_document_item
          WHERE SalesOrganization = lt_sales_document_item-salesorganization
          AND DistributionChannel = lt_sales_document_item- 
                                            distributionchannel
          AND Product = lt_sales_document_item-Material
          AND Customer =  lt_sales_document_item-SoldToParty
          INTO TABLE lt_Customer_material.

       LOOP AT ls_proxy_structure-invoice-invoice_line
         ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_invoice_line>).

         DATA(ls_item_posnr) = <ls_invoice_line>-id-base-base-content.
         DATA(lv_item) = <ls_invoice_line>-item-sellers_item_identification- 
                         id-base-base-content.

         READ TABLE lt_sales_document_item INTO DATA(ls_sales_document_item)
           WITH KEY salesdocumentitem = ls_item_posnr  
                    Material = lv_item.

*        If Customer material is found in i_salesdocumentitem.
         IF ls_sales_document_item-MaterialByCustomer IS NOT INITIAL. 
           <ls_invoice_line>-item-buyers_item_identification-id-base-base- 
            content = ls_sales_document_item-materialbycustomer.

         ELSE.
*        Check item information in Customer Material CDS view
          READ TABLE lt_customer_material
          INTO DATA(ls_customer_material)
          WITH KEY Product = lv_item.

          IF sy-subrc = 0.
*           Fill <BuyersItemIdentification> tag
            <ls_invoice_line>-item-buyers_item_identification-id-base-base- 
            content = ls_customer_material-MaterialByCustomer. 
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDCASE.
    cs_output_data = ls_proxy_structure.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

 

 

Result

When you display the XML file of the electronic document in the eDocument Cockpit or Manage Electronic Documents app, you will see that the <BuyersItemIdentification> tag is now filled with the customer material number:

Feel free to reach out if you have any questions or need further assistance with the implementation.

Happy coding!

Related blog posts:

  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Adding Atta... - SAP Community
  • How to prevent creation of Electronic Documents fo... - SAP Community

entry.title: India Business: Keeping Backup of books of Account Leveraging SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
entry.content:
Enhanced Record-Keeping Regulations for Indian Businesses

As of August 5, 2022, Indian businesses must adhere to new record-keeping regulations introduced by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA). These regulations, outlined in the Companies (Accounts) Fourth Amendment Rules, 2022, mandate that companies ensure their electronic records are accessible within India at all times. Additionally, businesses are required to maintain a daily backup of their books of account data within the country.

Key Requirements

  • Accessibility: Electronic records must be accessible within India at all times.
  • Daily Backup: A daily backup of books of account data must be stored within India.

Leveraging SAP S/4HANA for Compliance

For businesses using SAP S/4HANA, compliance can be streamlined by utilizing the Statutory Reporting of SAP Document and Reporting Compliance. This tool helps users extract the necessary books of account data from their SAP S/4HANA systems, ensuring they meet the new regulatory requirements.

Guidance and Support

For detailed guidance on how to implement these changes, SAP has provided SAP Note 3443920 .

Important Note: Please be aware that the information provided here is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal or regulatory advice. Businesses should consult with their legal advisors to ensure full compliance with the new regulations.

If you have any questions or need further assistance, feel free to reach out. We're here to help you navigate these new requirements smoothly.

Karthiheyini Subbiah

entry.title: New fields in the EFD – Reinf Report (Brazil) Migration Object
entry.content:
Hello,

Hello SAP Community,

I am excited to share some important updates with you! The EFD – Reinf report (Brazil) migration object has recently been enhanced to include new fields that will assist you in migrating your data. Each of these new fields is associated with specific EFD-Reinf events, as listed below.

For the R-1050 event:

  • Classification of the Related Entity (TP_ENT_LIG)
  • CNPJ of the Related Entity (CNPJ_LIG)

For the R-4099 event:

  • Closing or Reopening Indicative (FECH_RET)

For the R-4080 event:

  • CNPJ of the Source Paying the Income (CNPJ_FONT)

For the R-4010 event:

  •  Beneficiary's CPF (CPF_BENEF)
  • Beneficiary's Name (NM_BENEF)
  • ID of Additional File Sent by Benefic. (IDE_ADIC_BENEF)

For the R-4020 event:

  • Beneficiary's Name (NM_BENEF)
  • Beneficiary's CNPJ (CNPJ_BENEF)
  • ID of Additional File Sent by Benefic. (IDE_ADIC_BENEF)

For the R-4040 event:

  • ID of Additional File Sent by Benefic. (IDE_ADIC_BENEF)

To learn more about these updates and stay informed with our latest news, I recommend you visit the following SAP Help Portal pages:

  • About the migration object: EFD - Reinf report (Brazil)
  • EFD-Reinf Report product assistance page. 
  • Available Migration Objects

 

We would love your feedback. If you have any questions or suggestions for an upcoming post, please leave your comment below. 

See you next time!

entry.title: Getting Digital Certificate to Configure Communication Settings for Argentina
entry.content:
Note: The steps and processes described here are accurate as of the time of writing, but it's essential to stay updated with any changes on the Argentinian tax authority portal that may affect this procedure. 

Hello! 

In this blog post, we will guide you through configuring the communication settings in the Manage Communication Settings - External Parties app in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition.  

To establish a connection between your business system and the Argentinian tax authority, you need to obtain a digital certificate from the tax authority portal and upload it to the app. You get the digital certificate ready for use before configuring communication settings in the cloud edition. 

Prerequisites 

You have performed the necessary steps to exchange electronic invoices for Argentina using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition. For more information, see Integrating e-Invoices for Argentina, Poland, Romania, Serbia, or Slovakia with SAP S/4HANA Cloud. 

Step 1: Getting the Digital Certificate from the Tax Authority 

You obtain a digital certificate in the Argentinian tax authority portal. For more information, see Manual del Usuario del WSASS and WSAA Manual Del Desarrollador (Chapter 5). 

Step 2: Create Communication Settings 

You create a communication setting using your individual taxpayer identification number (CUIT). See WSASS: Cómo adherirse al servicio for more information. 

Note: Before performing this step, you must first activate the E-Invoices (AR) process in the Activate Processes app. 

Step 3: Add an Authentication Setting  

For the communication type created in step 2, add an authentication setting as follows: 

  1. Select the Key Pair X.509 type. 
  1. Upload the certificate you generated from the Argentinian tax authority portal. 
  1. Enter the password you received along with the certificate.  

Note: SAP supports soft certificates only, roaming certificates are not supported. The digital certificate extensions supported are PFX and P12.

More Information 

Suppose that your company has several company codes representing headquarters and subsidiary units. In this case, you can choose to use only one digital certificate, which is registered with the CUIT of headquarters or a leading subsidiary.  

Also remember that a digital certificate has an expiration date. You must renew your certificate when it is about to expire, otherwise, you'll encounter the following errors: 

  • Certificate is not valid according to the chain of trust validation or has been issued by an untrusted certificate authority. 
  • Signing time is not within validity period. 

We hope that you find this information useful. Feel free to leave a comment on this blog or follow SAP Document and Reporting Compliance | SAP Community.

entry.title: Receiving Electronic Invoices Through the Peppol Network in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management
entry.content:
In this blog, we will explore how SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management can process electronic invoices received through the Peppol network.

As of January 2025, a new legal mandate in Germany requires businesses to be able to receive invoices electronically from their suppliers. For more information about the law in general, check out this blog New B2B electronic invoicing law in Germany: What you need to know.

Multiple Solutions Available

While this blog focuses on handling electronic invoices through the Peppol network, it's important to note that there are other solutions available. SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management also supports electronic invoices received by email. There are additional SAP offerings for handling electronic invoices received from your supplier. To get a complete list, check out the Supported Business Scenarios for Germany.

For more information on some of these alternatives, please refer to the following blogs: 

  • Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, or SAP ERP
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices Through the Peppol Network in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices through the Peppol network in SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, or SAP ERP

Overview of the Process

The process of receiving  invoices through the Peppol network in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management involves several key steps:

  1. Receiving the Invoice
    Your supplier sends an invoice through the Peppol network and the invoice is received at your receiver access point in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition.
  2. Validating and Mapping the XML
    SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition validates the electronic invoice and maps it to the standard format used in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management.
  3. Verifying and Submitting Posting File
    If manual adjustments are needed, such as correcting missing or incorrect fields, you can edit the invoice using the Manage Supplier Invoices Centrally app    . Then you post the posting file to the connected business system.
  4. Further Processing
    An invoice for the received electronic document is created in your connected business system and you can further process it.

For more details about the process, see Receive Invoices from Peppol Network.

How to Get Started?

To establish the communication between the two services, you need to do some configurations as follows:

  • In SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management
    Create an instance of the SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management service with the inbound-drc service plan and generate a service key for this instance. See Service Keys.
  • In SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition
  1. Set up the connection from SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition to SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management. This connection facilitates the Peppol Exchange process. See Setting Up Connectivity.
  2. Specify client credentials required for your system to communicate with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition using OAuth 2.0 client authentication. See Creating Service Bindings Using OAuth 2.0 Client Authentication for Invoices Received Through the Peppol Network.

    Note: If you are interested in processing electronic invoices received by email, refer to Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management.

By following these steps, you can ensure compliance with Germany's new electronic invoicing law and streamline your invoicing processes using SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management.

entry.title: Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, or SAP ERP
entry.content:
In this blog, we will explore how SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, or SAP ERP can help businesses comply with the new German regulation by receiving electronic invoices by email. As of January 2025, a new legal mandate in Germany requires businesses to be able to receive invoices electronically from their suppliers. For more information about the law in general, check out this blog New B2B electronic invoicing law in Germany: What you need to know.

 

Multiple solutions available

While this blog focuses on SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP ERP, it's important to note that there are alternative SAP offerings for handling electronic invoices received from your supplier. To get a complete list, check out the Supported Business Scenarios.

For more information on some of these alternatives, please refer to the following blogs: 

  • Receiving Electronic Invoices Through the Peppol Network in SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition or SAP ERP
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices Through the Peppol Network in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices Through the Peppol Network in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management

 

Overview of the process in SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, or SAP ERP

German businesses have two primary options to manage electronic invoices received by email.

Option 1: Automatic extraction, upload, and electronic document creation

  1. Receiving the Invoice: Your supplier sends an invoice or credit note to your SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, or SAP ERP system by email. 
  2. Extracting the XML File: The business system extracts the XML file from the email.
  3. Creating an Electronic Document: The business system creates an electronic document in the eDocument Cockpit or the Manage Electronic Documents app.
  4. Submitting for Validation: The next step is to submit the electronic document in the eDocument Cockpit or the Manage Electronic Documents app for validation to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition. This step can be automated as well by using the eDocument Background Processing report.
    • Submit button in the Manage Electronic Document app
  5. Validating the XML File: The cloud edition validates the invoice and returns the validation result. It also creates an easy-to-read HTML version of the invoice and returns it to the business system.
  6. Review Status: You can view the new status after the validation as well as the HTML invoice in the business system in the eDocument Cockpit or the Manage Electronic Documents app.
    • Display HTML button in the Manage Electronic Document app
  7. Further Processing: If you’ve implemented an incoming automation solution, you can forward the invoice from the eDocument Cockpit or the Manage Electronic Documents app to the incoming automation solution for further processing. This step can be automated.
    • Create Incoming Invoice button in the Manage Electronic Document app

Option 2: Manual extraction, upload, and electronic document creation

  1. Receiving the Invoice: Your supplier sends an invoice or credit note to you by email. 
  2. Extracting the XML File: You extract the XML file from the email to your local system.
  3. Uploading the XML File to Business System: You use the Upload Tool for Incoming Messages report in SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition or SAP ERP to upload the file.
    • Upload Tool for Incoming Messages report
  4. Creating an Electronic Document: You use the eDocument Inbound Message Handler report in SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition or SAP ERP to create an electronic document that shows up in the eDocument Cockpit or the Manage Electronic Documents app.
    • eDocument Inbound Message Handler report
  5. Submitting for Validation: In the eDocument Cockpit or the Manage Electronic Documents app, you submit the electronic document for validation to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition.
    • Submit button in the Manage Electronic Document app
  6. Validating the XML File: The cloud edition validates the invoice and returns the validation result. It also creates an easy-to-read HTML version of the invoice and returns it to the business system.
  7. Reviewing Status: You can view the new status after the validation as well as the HTML invoice in the business system in the eDocument Cockpit or the Manage Electronic Documents app.
    • Display HTML button in the Manage Electronic Document app
  8. Further: If you’ve implemented an incoming automation solution, you can forward the invoice from the eDocument Cockpit or the Manage Electronic Documents app to the incoming automation solution for further processing. This step can be automated. For more information, check Implementation of an Incoming Automation Solution.
    • Create Incoming Invoice button in the Manage Electronic Document app

For more information about the tasks you can perform in the eDocument Cockpit or the Manage Electronic Documents app for the electronic invoice you’ve received by email, see:

  • For SAP S/4HANA, or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition:  Validating and Processing Electronic Invoices Received by E-Mail Using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition.
  • For SAP ERP: Validating and Processing Electronic Invoices Received by E-Mail Using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition

 

More information

For details about the end-to-end solution, please refer to the following information:  

  • For SAP S/4HANA, or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, see:
    • Electronic Supplier Invoices (Receipt by E-Mail)
    • Integration
  • For SAP ERP, see:
    • Electronic Supplier Invoices (Receipt by E-Mail)
    • Integration

The integration of the Peppol Exchange process is one part of the integration steps for the end-to-end solution. If you are interested in why the Peppol Exchange process of the cloud edition is relevant for the receiving of invoices by email, check out this blog: Email Solution for Germany: Understanding Use of the Peppol Exchange Process in the Cloud Edition. In addition, we want to make you aware that if you already have done the integration of the Peppol Exchange process for the receiving of electronic invoices through the Peppol network, you don’t need to perform the implementation steps again to get to the email validation solution for SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition or SAP ERP.

 

By leveraging SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP ERP, businesses in Germany can seamlessly transition to electronic invoicing, ensuring compliance with the new legal requirements while optimizing their invoicing processes.

entry.title: Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
In this blog, we will explore how SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition can help businesses comply with the new German regulation by receiving electronic invoices by email. As of January 2025, a new legal mandate in Germany requires businesses to be able to receive invoices electronically from their suppliers. For more information about the law in general, check out this blog New B2B electronic invoicing law in Germany: What you need to know.

Multiple solutions available

While this blog focuses on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for handling electronic invoices received by email, it's important to note that there are alternative SAP offerings for handling electronic invoices received from your supplier. To get a complete list, check out the Supported Business Scenarios.

For more information on some of these alternatives, please refer to the following blogs: 

  • Receiving Electronic Invoices Through the Peppol Network in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition or SAP ERP
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices Through the Peppol Network in SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition or SAP ERP
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices Through the Peppol Network in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management

     

Overview of the process in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

The process of receiving electronic invoices by email in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition involves several steps. Here's a detailed breakdown of the procedure: 

  1. Receiving the Invoice: Your supplier sends an invoice or credit note to you by email. 
  2. Extracting the XML File: You extract the XML file from the email to your local system.
  3. Uploading the XML File to Business System: You use the Upload Incoming Messages app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to upload the file.
    • Upload Incoming Messages app
  4. Creating an Electronic Document: You use the EDocument Inbound Message Handler app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to create an electronic document that shows up in the Manage Electronic Documents app.
    • Inbound Messages app
  5. Submitting for Validation: In the Manage Electronic Documents app, you submit the document for validation to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition.
    • Submit button in the Manage Electronic Document app
  6. Validating the XML File: The cloud edition validates the invoice and returns the validation result. It also creates an easy-to-read HTML version of the invoice and returns it to the business system.
  7. Reviewing Status: You can view the new status after the validation as well as the HTML invoice in the business system in the Manage Electronic Documents app. In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition further processing of the electronic document is not planned for invoices received by email. If you want to further process the invoice, use the solution Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management.
    • Display HTML button in the Manage Electronic Document app

For more information about the tasks you can perform in the eDocument Cockpit for the electronic invoice you’ve received by email, see Validating Electronic Invoices Received by E-Mail Using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition.

 

More information

For details about the end-to-end solution, please refer to the following information: 

  • Electronic Supplier Invoices (Receipt by E-Mail) 
  • Validating Electronic Supplier Invoices Using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition

The integration of the Peppol Exchange process is one part of the integration steps for the end-to-end solution. If you are interested in why the Peppol Exchange process of the cloud edition is relevant for the receiving of invoices by email, check out this blog: Email Solution for Germany: Understanding Use of the Peppol Exchange Process in the Cloud Edition. In addition, we want to make you aware that if you already have done the integration of the Peppol Exchange process for the Receiving of electronic invoices through the Peppol network, you don’t need to perform all implementation steps again to get to the email solution for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

 

By leveraging SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, businesses in Germany can seamlessly transition to electronic invoicing, ensuring compliance with the new legal requirements while optimizing their invoicing processes.

entry.title: Receiving Electronic Invoices Through the Peppol Network in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
In this blog, we will explore how SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition can help businesses comply with the new German regulation by receiving electronic invoices through the Peppol network. As of January 2025, a new legal mandate in Germany requires businesses to be able to receive invoices electronically from their suppliers. For more information about the law in general, check out this blog New B2B electronic invoicing law in Germany: What you need to know.

 

Multiple solutions available

While this blog focuses on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, it's important to note that there are alternative SAP offerings for handling electronic invoices received from your supplier. To get a complete list, check out the Supported Business Scenarios.

For more information on some of these alternatives, please refer to the following blogs: 

  • Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition or SAP ERP
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices Through the Peppol Network in SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private E...
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices Through the Peppol Network in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management

 

Overview of the process in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

The process of receiving electronic invoices through the Peppol network in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition involves several steps. Here's a detailed breakdown of the procedure: 

  1. Receiving the Invoice: Your supplier sends an invoice or credit note to you through the Peppol network. 
  2. Receiving the invoice in the receiver access point: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition acts as the receiver access point.
  3. Validating the electronic document:  The cloud edition runs business and technical checks and returns a delivery notification to the sender access point.
  4. Creating an Electronic Document: The cloud edition forwards the invoice S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition where the electronic document is created in the Manage Electronic Documents app. Further processing of the electronic document is not planned for supplier invoices. If you want to further process the invoice, use the solution  Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management.
    • Electronic Document in the Manage Electronic Documents app

For more information about the tasks you can perform in the Manage Electronic Documents app for the electronic invoice you’ve received by email, see Processing Supplier Invoices.

 

More information

To implement the end-to-end solution, you must:

  • have integrated SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition and have completed the onboarding activities. See Integrating Peppol Exchange with SAP S/4HANA Cloud.
  • have performed the required configuration activities. See Configuration Options for Key Users.

 

By leveraging SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, businesses in Germany can seamlessly transition to electronic invoicing, ensuring compliance with the new legal requirements while optimizing their invoicing processes.

entry.title: Receiving Electronic Invoices Through the Peppol Network in SAP S/4HANA or SAP ERP
entry.content:
In this blog, we will explore how SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, or SAP ERP can help businesses comply with the new German regulation by receiving electronic invoices through the Peppol network. As of January 2025, a new legal mandate in Germany requires businesses to be able to receive invoices electronically from their suppliers. For more information about the law in general, check out this blog New B2B electronic invoicing law in Germany: What you need to know.

 

Multiple solutions available

While this blog focuses on SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP ERP, it's important to note that there are alternative SAP offerings for handling electronic invoices received from your supplier. To get a complete list, check out the Supported Business Scenarios.

For more information on some of these alternatives, please refer to the following blogs: 

  • Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition or SAP ERP
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices Through the Peppol Network in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices Through the Peppol Network in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management

 

Overview of the process in SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, or SAP ERP

The process of receiving electronic invoices through the Peppol network in SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP ERP involves several steps. Here's a detailed breakdown of the procedure: 

  1. Receiving the Invoice: Your supplier sends an invoice or credit note to you through the Peppol network. 
  2. Receiving the invoice in the receiver access point: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition acts as the receiver access point.
  3. Validating the electronic document: The cloud edition runs business and technical checks and returns a delivery notification to the sender access point.
  4. Creating an Electronic Document: The cloud edition forwards the invoice to the eDocument Cockpit or the Manage Electronic Documents app in SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP ERP, where an electronic document is created.
    • Electronic Document in the Manage Electronic Documents app
  5. Further Processing: If you’ve implemented an incoming automation solution, the system forwards the invoice from the eDocument Cockpit or the Manage Electronic Documents app to the incoming automation solution for further processing.

For more information about the tasks you can perform in the eDocument Cockpit or the Manage Electronic Documents app for the electronic invoice you’ve received by email, see:

  • For SAP S/4HANA, or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition: Processing Electronic Supplier Invoices
  • For SAP ERP: Germany Incoming eInvoice 

 

More information

To implement the end-to-end solution, you must:

  • Have integrated the Peppol Exchange process for the cloud edition of SAP Document and Reporting Compliance and done the onboarding activities. See:
    • For SAP S/4HANA, or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition: Integrating Peppol Exchange with SAP S/4HANA
    • For SAP ERP:  Integrating Peppol Exchange with SAP ERP
  • Have done the customizing steps. See:
    • For SAP S/4HANA, or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition: Customizing
    • For SAP ERP: Customizing

By leveraging SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP ERP, businesses in Germany can seamlessly transition to electronic invoicing, ensuring compliance with the new legal requirements while optimizing their invoicing processes.

entry.title: Email Solution for Germany: Understanding Use of the Peppol Exchange Process in the Cloud Edition
entry.content:
For Germany, SAP offers multiple solutions to handle invoices received from your suppliers by email*, all leveraging SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition. In this blog, we'll clarify the role of the cloud edition in the process of receiving electronic invoices by email.

 

The Role of the Cloud Edition

In the end-to-end process, the cloud edition performs the validation of the electronic invoices. You might notice a difference compared to using the cloud edition for sending invoices through the Peppol network, where the cloud edition functions as an access point.

 

Integration

To actually use the cloud edition for the validation, you must implement the Peppol Exchange process. The detailed steps are described on the SAP Help Portal: Check out the Supported Business Scenarios, and find the right implementation steps for your business system in the Links to Integration Steps column.

 

*Here are links to blogs about the different solutions to handle invoices received from your suppliers by email:

  • Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition or SAP ERP
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management

entry.title: Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management
entry.content:
In this blog, we will explore how SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management can process electronic invoices received by email.

As of January 2025, a new legal mandate in Germany requires businesses to be able to receive invoices electronically from their suppliers. For more information about the law in general, check out this blog New B2B electronic invoicing law in Germany: What you need to know.

Multiple solutions available

While this blog focuses on handling electronic invoices received by email, it's important to note that there are other solutions available. SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management also supports electronic invoices received through the Peppol network. There are additional SAP offerings for handling electronic invoices received from your supplier. To get a complete list, check out the Supported Business Scenarios for Germany.

For more information on some of these alternatives, refer to the following blogs: 

  • Receiving Electronic Invoices Through the Peppol Network in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition or SAP ERP
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices Through the Peppol Network in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices Through the Peppol network in in SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, or SAP ERP

Overview of the Process

As a company in Germany, you have two options to manage electronic invoices received by email with SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management.

Option 1: Automatic extraction from the email and upload of the XML using SAP Build Process Automation.

  1. File Extraction from Email and Upload:
    You use the Supplier Invoice File Extraction from Email and Upload to SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management task automation to automate the extraction and upload of supplier invoices, which you receive as an email attachment, to your SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management. See Supplier Invoice File Extraction from Email and Upload to SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management (6JX).
    As a result, the system automatically creates a draft invoice for each uploaded file, attaching the file to the draft invoice.
  2. Validation and Mapping
    The business system forwards the electronic invoice to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition.  The system validates the invoice and maps it to the standard format used in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management. As a result, if the invoice is complete and valid, its status changes to "Parked and Completed."
  3. Verifying and Submitting Posting File
    If manual adjustments are needed, such as correcting missing or incorrect fields, you can edit the invoice using the Manage Supplier Invoices Centrally app    . Then you submit the posting file to the connected business system. This will trigger an approval workflow.
  4. Further Processing
    An invoice for the received electronic document is created in your connected business system, and you can further process it.

Option 2: Manual extraction from the email and manual upload of the XML file in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management.

  1. Extracting the XML File
    You received an email with an attached electronic invoice file, and you extract the XML file to your local system.
  2. Uploading the File
    You upload the XML file to SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management using the Upload Supplier Invoices Centrally app. For more information, see Upload Supplier Invoices Centrally. As a result, the system automatically creates a draft invoice for each uploaded file, attaching the file to the draft invoice.
  3. Validation and Mapping
    The system forwards the electronic invoice to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition.  The cloud edition functionalities validate the invoice and map it to the standard format used in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management. As a result, if the invoice is complete and valid, its status changes to "Parked and Completed."
  4. Verifying and Submitting Posting File
    If manual adjustments are needed, such as correcting missing or incorrect fields, you can edit the invoice using the Manage Supplier Invoices Centrally app. Then you submit the posting file to the connected business system. This will trigger an approval workflow.
  5. Further Processing
    An invoice for the received electronic document is created in your connected business system, and you can further process it.

How to get started?

To be able to use the scenarios you need to do the following configurations in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management:

  1. Set up the connection from SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition. Configuration steps described on SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management. See Setting Up Connectivity.
  2. Activate data extraction of the XML file with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition in the Manage Processing Services app. See Activate Data Extraction of XML Electronic Invoice with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition.

To complete the integration, you must do some configurations in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition:

  1. Set up the connection from SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition to SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management. See Setting Up Connectivity.
  2. Specify client credentials required to enable your business system to communicate with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition, using OAuth 2.0 client authentication. See Creating Service Bindings Using OAuth 2.0 Client Authentication for Invoices Received by E-Mail (Germany Only)

    Note:     If you have already integrated SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition to receive invoices through the Peppol network, you only need to create the additional service binding and use the other setting as they are already configured.

For information about accepting and processing electronic invoices received through the Peppol network, please refer to Receiving Electronic Invoices Through the Peppol Network in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management.

By leveraging SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management, businesses in Germany can seamlessly transition to accepting electronic invoices, ensuring compliance with the new legal requirements while optimizing their invoicing processes.

entry.title: Customizing Your PDF with the Transparency Information Required by the Argentinean Tax Authority
entry.content:
Hello! 
 
If you carry out sales, work placements or provision of services to final consumers in Argentina using print character B, according to resolution 5614, as of January 1st, 2025, you must indicate the final amount that the final consumer must pay in the publication of the prices of the respective goods or services.

According to the tax authority, this information is displayed in a new box containing the following information:  

Title 

Description 

Régimen de Transparencia Fiscal al Consumidor (Ley 27.743) 

Fixed title 

IVA Contenido $/USD 

Total VAT of the document in document currency (use the symbol $ for local currency or ISO currency code for foreign currencies like USD).  

In case you have more than one VAT rate in the document, this line should show the summarization of all IVA amounts. 

Otros Impuestos Nacionales Indirectos $/USD 

Total of other national taxes (not VAT), for example Internal tax (use the symbol $ for local currency or ISO currency code for foreign currencies like USD). In SAP, we have identified this kind of tax as “internal tax” 

Note that Otros Impuestos Nacionales Indirectos are not applied to all companies. For more information, see Transparencia fiscal: ARCA reglamenta el régimen que obliga a discriminar el IVA en las facturas and Resolución General 5614/2024. 

With this blog post, we will guide you with examples on how to comply with this legal change if you use PDF forms in the eDocument Cockpit for Argentina with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.  

Please note that the steps mentioned are suggestions, indications, and examples. You may use your own logic if what is provided does not apply to your needs. 

Prerequisites:

You have Adobe LiveCycle Designer on your computer and know how to design forms with this tool. 

You have assigned the Extensibility (SAP_CORE_BC_EXT) authorization role for your user. 

Procedure:

  1. Access the Maintain Form Templates app. 
  2. Go to the Predelivered Templates tab and search for the following PDF form: 
    Document Type:Print Char A, B, E
    Form Template Name:EDO_AR_PRINT
    Data Source:EDOC_AR_PRINT_SRV_03
  3. Open the form template and select Download. 
  4. Extract the downloaded ZIP file and open the .xdp file in the Adobe Livecycle Desginer. 
  5. In the Adobe LifeCycle Designer, choose the Master Pages tab.  
  6. From the Object Library, insert a Text Field to give the table its title, Régimen de Transparencia Fiscal al Consumidor. 
  7. From the Object Library, insert a table to your form by selecting Table. 
  8. Create a simple table with 2 columns and 2 rows. 
  9. Name the rows by double-clicking a cell. Consider the required texts, as follows:
    • First row: IVA Contenido 
    • Second row: Otros Impuestos Nacionales Indirectos

  10. To fill in the information for IVA Contenido, perform as follows:  
  1. Double-click the respective cell and navigate from Object > Cell and set its type as Text Field.  
  2. To bind the data, you have different options, choose the one that best suits your scenario.  
  • Use Javascript logic to get your specific values.
  • Choose the Binding tab and, in the Data Binding field, paste the following:  $.HEADER.VATSet.VAT[0].Amount  
    The [0] index means you are getting the first row of the referenced table. 

11. The value for Otros Impuestos Nacionales is already available in the Otros Tributos table. To retrieve the value, perform as follows: 

  1. Double-click the respective cell and navigate from Object > Cell and set its type as Text Field. 
  2. To bind the data, you have different options, choose the one best suits your scenario: 
  • Choose the Binding tab and, in the Data Binding field, paste the following: $.HEADER.OTHER_TAXSet.OTHER_TAX[0].Amount  

  • Use Javascript logic to get your specific values, for example:  

var description = this.resolveNode("Page1.other_taxset.item_other_tax[0].description").rawValue; 

  

if(description == 'Impto.interno ventas'){ 
  this.rawValue = this.resolveNode("Page1.other_taxset.item_other_tax[0].amount").rawValue; 
} 

12. Save your changes. 
13. Access the Maintain Form Templates app. 
14. On the Custom Templates tab, choose New. Create a custom template for your form by making the following settings: 

  • Form Template Name: Enter a name for your form template. 
  • Language: Select the language of your form template. 
  • Select Form Template File: Upload the file that you've created in Adobe LiveCycle Designer. 
  • Data Source: EDOC_AR_PRINT_SRV_03 
  • Form Type: Choose Content. 

15. Save your form template. 
16. In your configuration environment, use the search function to open the Configure Printing Form for eInvoice (103277) configuration activity and assign your custom form template to the relevant eDocument type and branch.  

You can now open the eDocument Cockpit and submit your document. The new PDF form template now contains the Régimen de Transparencia Fiscal al Consumidor (Ley 27.743) box and its information.

For more information, go to Document and Reporting Compliance for Argentina on the SAP Help Portal.

We hope that you find this information useful. Feel free to leave a comment on this blog or follow SAP Document and Reporting Compliance | SAP Community.

entry.title: 亮点前瞻之 SAP ERP公有云2502中国本地化发布
entry.content:
新春的脚步渐近。回望2024年，我们心中充满了感慨与自豪。在这个快速变化的数字化时代，我们以创新为引擎，以协作为翅膀，迈出坚定的步伐。在这一年中，SAP全球化部门根据中国市场的呼声，发布了覆盖了税务、资金、报表和法规等各个关键性业务模块的本地化增强功能。

        以下是相关信息的参考链接：

  1. SAP ERP公有云2408大中国区本地化亮点一览
  2. 本地化发布：SAP S/4HANA 上的消息推送服务
  3. 中国跨境数据传输相关法规简要概述

        接下来，将为您介绍近期即将发布的ERP 公有云2502版本关于中国本地化的主要亮点。 

中国本地化报表更新

中国应收/应付账款帐龄报表功能更新

        自该版本起，用户可以在根据公司代码级别检索并得到所有未清应收账款/应付账款数据后，通过点击新增的“小计开启/关闭”按键，关闭小计开关，然后双击总计行以实现一次性查看所有行详细信息。该功能旨在为财务用户拥有更高的灵活性查看自己所需的报表信息，方便其工作的便利性。

中国现金流量表（基于SAP DRC）功能更新

        自该版本起，基于SAP Document and Reporting Compliance （SAP DRC）的中国现金流量表上，将支持将往期手工调整结果带入当期计算，从而该报表的用户在后续期间或者当期运行报表时，可以通过勾选“包括手动调整项目”选项，在当前报表中体现往期手工调整的结果。该功能帮助财务用户、相关管理层及审计单位更好地追溯报表历史、丰富报表的监测维度。

        同时，自该版本起，基于抵销科目确定方案的中国现金流量表（基于SAP DRC）中，关于抵消科目的计算将不再占用ACDOCA-GKONT字段；同时启用新字段OFFSTG_DOCLN存放拆分信息。通过不再调用GKONT这一逻辑调整，保证了GKONT数值的独立性，从而进一步保证核心财务报表的功能性和准确性。

 

S/4HANA消息推送与社交媒体集成服务功能更新

        基于SAP 业务技术平台（BTP）的S/4HANA消息推送与社交媒体的集成，可以及时向关键用户推送来自S/4HANA系统发布的工作流消息、预警事件消息。

        在本期的发布中，提供以下新功能（包括但不限于）：用户可直接在手机端社交媒体内，执行工作流操作，如审批、拒绝，管理和优化日常业务流程，提高工作效率，保证关键用户可以及时了解S/4HANA重要业务流程状态以及重要事件通知，并及时采取下一步业务行动。

        由于该服务能大力提高生产效率，降低沟通协作成本，优化用户体验，同时以其极低的上线成本，一直受到众多客户的青睐。

 

中国增值税发票管理(基于SAP DRC)功能更新

        自该版本起，SAP中国本地化税务管理方案有了更加丰富、灵活的功能。

        中国销项税发票管理方案，实现了与BRIM/CI的集成，从而CI里的发票可以通过销项税发票管理与金税系统进行数据交互；优化了合并算法，解决了当参与合并的发票的行项目数量过多时出现的部分发票不能继续被合并的情况及对应位差处理异常的问题；与国家电子发票服务平台上的数电发票批量开票的上载模版进行了同步更新。

 

        中国进项税发票管理方案中则有以下增强：

        在Fiori “计划增值税进项作业”中新增了与发票自动抵扣勾选相关的模板，允许用户按一定条件，将符合申报条件的发票数据自动发送至税局端完成相关抵扣勾选；提供“发票申报结果重新同步”的作业模板，实现将未返回的税局处理结果重新自动同步至S/4 HANA；支持数电发票文件或者URL同步；支持实时获取已有发票的最新状态；在SAP Integration Suite中发布了的一整套直连标准模板Integration Flow，用户可以基于该模板进行客制化开发，与合作伙伴实现在线交互，更多细节请参考 Generic Template Iflow.

 

中国本地化资金更新

审批流程的功能更新

        自SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2502起，Fiori “管理付款申请“将结合新的消息控制SSCUI，提供更便捷、更弹性的审批功能，包括审批状态提醒、审批状态追踪、审批状态通知等个性化功能。

 

        展望2025年，前路生机勃勃，充满机遇和挑战。SAP全球化部门一如既往，为您在云时代的合规运营及本地化管理，赋能未来。让我们以满怀信心和期待的心情，继续携手，迎接新的一年。

关于S/4HANA 2502中国本地化功能的技术详情，欢迎请参考链接。

entry.title: Enhancing the eDocument Cockpit with BAdI EDOC_CUSTOM_FIELDS_CLOUD
entry.content:
In this blog post we’ll describe how you can use the BAdI method FILL_EDOC_CUSTOM_FIELDS of BAdI EDOC_CUSTOM_FIELDS_CLOUD to extend the eDocument Cockpit (EDOC_COCKPIT) with custom fields. This enhancement allows you to add your own custom fields to display data from various source documents like billing, accounting and delivery.

Please note that these customization settings apply only to new electronic documents generated after the implementation.

Prerequisites

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Developer Extensibility

Steps to be Implemented

  • Creation of the custom field
  • Implementation of the custom logic

Creation of the Custom Field

  1. Go to Custom Fields app.
  2. Select Create to add a new custom field. In this example, we’ll add the Total Weight field. If you select the type Quantity with Unit, the Unit of Weight field is also created.

     3. Define the labels of the custom field and choose Publish.

The publishing process takes some time. Once it's published, you can move on to the implementation steps in ADT (ABAP Development Tools).

Implementation Steps in ABAP Development Tools (ADT)

  1. In your ABAP project, go to the enhancement spot ES_DOCUMENT_CLOUD (path: package GLO-EDO > Enhancements > Enhancement Spots).
  2. Right-click ES_EDOCUMENT_CLOUD and select New BAdI Enhancement Implementation.
  3. Enter a package, a name, and a description.
  4. Choose Add BAdI Implementation and select EDOC_CUSTOM_FIELDS_CLOUD.
  5. Create a BAdI filter for process.
  6. Choose Implementing class to create a new implementation class. The new implementation class automatically implements the interfaces "if_badi_interfaces" and "if_edoc_custom_fields_cloud".
  7. After generating the class, open the interface method and press F3 to view its parameters. To display the documentation, select the BAdI definition, press F2, and select "read more".
  8. Activate your BAdI implementation.

Example Implementation

In the following example, we’ll fill the Total weight and the Unit of weight of the delivery to the custom field “yy1_totalweight_edo” and “yy1_totalweight_edou”  which was created in the previous step.

Coding:

 

CLASS zcl_edoc_custom_fields_cloud_t DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.

    INTERFACES if_badi_interface .
    INTERFACES if_edoc_custom_fields_cloud .
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_edoc_custom_fields_cloud_t IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD if_edoc_custom_fields_cloud~fill_edoc_custom_fields.

*For Process Mexico Delivery Note - Filling btgew and gewei ( Total Weight and Unit of Weight )
    IF process = 'MXDELN'.
        IF source_data-delivery_header-btgew IS NOT INITIAL AND source_data-delivery_header-gewei IS NOT INITIAL.
          custom_fields-yy1_totalweight_edo = source_data-delivery_header-btgew.
          custom_fields-yy1_totalweight_edou = source_data-delivery_header-gewei.
        ENDIF.
    ENDIF.

  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

Result:

When you display a newly created Electronic document in the eDocument Cockpit, you’ll see that the Total Weight and the Unit of Weight of the delivery is filled.

Give us your feedback
To ensure that we’re covering what you need, please leave your feedback at the bottom of this blog post!
#SAPGoGlobal #SAPLocalization

entry.title: An Introduction to Documentation Versions on the SAP Help Portal for Functional Localization
entry.content:
I’m pleased to share the latest information on accessing documentation on the SAP Help Portal for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. In this blog post, I’ll explain how you can choose the right documentation version for your technical requirements and discuss the recent updates on the SAP Help Portal for maintenance phases.

Title image for the topic of documentation versions on the SAP Help Portal

I’ll be focusing today on the country/region-specific documentation for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition that is published through the Documentation Delivery with SAP Notes process, though there are useful tips here for everyone who browses version-specific content on the SAP Help Portal.

1. Choosing the Right Documentation Version for You

The SAP Help Portal is your number-one resource for information on SAP products. When navigating documentation, it’s important to verify that the version you’re browsing is correct for your solution. You can do this by checking the Version Selector drop-down menu, as shown in this example from the Local Versions page:

Drop-down version selection on the SAP Help Portal

Why do we recommend this version verification step? Well, you can access SAP Help Portal documentation by many different means, such as links from SAP Notes and What’s New topics, as well as from search engines. You may be directed to the highest version, which is not necessarily the same version implemented by your company. Different versions may have different scope functionality described in the documentation, so it’s best to always check this drop-down menu when accessing the SAP Help Portal.

2. New on the SAP Help Portal: Maintenance Information

You might have noticed that, when you access documentation for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, there’s a new information banner that specifies the maintenance phase:

Maintenance message now available on the SAP Help Portal

This information banner differentiates between two maintenance phases, Mainstream Maintenance and Extended Maintenance. If you are an SAP customer or partner with a valid SAP S-User ID, you can refer to the SAP Maintenance Phases resource for further details on this topic.

For the most recent documentation versions on the SAP Help Portal, you are informed that “documentation is updated with the support package stacks”. However, there is an important exception to this rule for country/region-specific updates delivered for functional localization.

3. Always Up-to-Date: Documentation Delivery for Functional Localization

For country/region-specific development delivered for functional localization, such as legal changes, a new process provides documentation updates between support package stacks. As detailed in the recent blog post on Documentation Delivery on the SAP Help Portal with SAP Notes, you now have access to SAP Help Portal documentation that reflects the latest information, regardless of the planned support package release dates.  

For both the mainstream and extended maintenance phases of SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, we’re glad to provide you with up-to-date information on how SAP meets the local legal requirement for your solution. 

To find more information on country/region-specific topics, I recommend the Localization topic page on the SAP Community. There you can find the latest news, resources, and upcoming events for SAP’s localized solutions. Remember, too, that you can always select the Create a Question option on the Localization topic page to engage with experts and resolve any doubts you may have:

Create a Question for Localization on the SAP Community

And for those of you who use SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, you can find more details on the SAP Help Portal and SAP Community in our dedicated blog series:

  • An Introduction to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Navigating the SAP Help Portal
  • An Introduction to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Engaging with the SAP Community

That’s it for today, though please let me know in the comments section if you have any questions. Your feedback is always appreciated.

Thank you and until next time,

Barnabas

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Send PDF with XML file via email to your business partners
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

The SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing options for Brazil allows you to send DANFE (NF-e) and DACTE (CT-e) in PDF format to your business partners via email. For SAP S/4HANA and SAP ERP, you use the NF-e/CT-e Monitor (J1BNFE) transaction to display the distribution status of the XML and PDF files for each electronic fiscal document.

Take a look at the updates in our What's New session.

Instructions to generate and submit the PDF with the XML file:

1 - Follow the manual instructions in SAP Note 3532295 to customize the attached form template (template.xdp) using the Adobe LiveCycle Designer. The file is a sample that can be used to create the template for the DANFE (NF-e). The maximum template size is set to 2 MB; remember to resize or compress images used in the file.

Editing the sample file in Adobe LiveCycle Designer.

2 - After creating a new template as an XDP file in the Adobe LiveCycle Designer, use a tool to encode the file to Base64 and save it to your computer. Since an XDP file has an XML format, you can copy its source code using a simple text editor before encoding it.

The content of an XDP file opened in a text editor.

3 - You must use an HTTP client or requests via curl to update the template in the service. In the following instructions, an HTTP client is used to create the requests (GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE) and set the endpoint to the URL https://nfe.cfapps.br10.hana.ondemand.com. Refer to the SAP Business Accelerator Hub for further details.

3.1 – The following process relies on token authentication (also known as bearer authentication), which is used over HTTPS (SSL). This authentication allows you to access URLs of our service. The client must send the token in the authorization header when making requests to protected resources:

 

Authorization: Bearer <token>

 

For more details about token authentication in the SAP BTP, check the following pages:

  • OAuth JWT Bearer Authentication
  • OAuth 2.0 – Understanding Token Exchange

3.2 - In the HTTP client, create a POST request with the following details:

  • URL: https://nfe.cfapps.br10.hana.ondemand.com/pdf-service/v1/templates
  • Method: POST
  • Auth: Input the generated authentication token to communicate with our cloud services. Use your clientId and clientSecret to get the token in the SAP BTP.

3.3 – In the Body, you must send the content using the HTTP client, filling in the following details:

  • Template name in templateName field, which can be whatever you want.
  • Electronic document type in documentType field, which must be filled in with “NFE” for DANFE or “CTE” for DACTE.
  • Action type in actionType field, filled in with “AUTHORIZE”.
  • Country in country field, filled in with “BR”.
  • Electronic document layout in the version field; this is an optional field, and in the example, it is filled in with “4.00”, which is the current NF-e (DANFE) layout version.
  • Environment Type in environmentType field; this is an optional field, and it is filled in with “HOMOLOGATION” for testing purposes, or “PRODUCTION”.
  • The content of the XDP template, encoded to Base64, must be pasted into xdpTemplate field.

 

{
	"templateName": "Name of the template",
	"documentType": "NFE",
	"actionType": "AUTHORIZE",
	"country": "BR",
	"version": "4.00",
	"environmentType": "HOMOLOGATION",
	"xdpTemplate":
"Content of the template encoded to Base64 here"
}

 

Template update using an HTTP client.

Note: There is a restriction for documentType, actionType, country, version, and environmentType; you should not send two or more templates that have the same values in these fields.

4 - Open the following pages in SAP Help to understand how to update the email addresses of your business partners and confirm that jobs are scheduled in your backend system.

  • Configuring Email Addresses to Send XML and PDF Files
  • Scheduling Jobs for NF-e, CT-e, NFS-e

5 - Submit an NF-e document to your business partner in the NF-e/CT-e Monitor (J1BNFE) transaction. When the document is authorized by SEFAZ, the Distribution Log column will be updated.

6 - Click on the icon in the Distribution Log column to check the details of the email sent in Distribution History > Log. For more details, check the Distribution Bounce Statuses page in SAP Help.

Steps in the NF-e/CT-e Monitor (J1BNFE) transaction.

The email with the PDF and XML files will be received by your business partner.

Note: After updating the template, it may take up to 15 minutes for the new version to be used, after which your business partners will receive the new PDF version and XML file via email.

You can sign up for email alerts when new SAP Notes are released or updated in the component LOD-LH-NFE (Outbound invoicing option for Brazil); just follow the instructions in SAP Knowledge Base Article 2171560.

Use the regulatory change manager tool to find out if a solution to a legal change is planned. Take a look at SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 and the regulatory change manager page to understand how to use this tool.

Thank you, and until next time,
Thiago


Tags: brasil, einvoice, einvoicing, e-invoicing, electronic invoice, electronic invoicing, electronic document, electronic documents, eletronic document, edocument, edoc, e-document, e-docs, e-doc, e-docs, drc, document compliance, nota fiscal eletrônica, eletronica, electronic nota fiscal, factura electrónica, conhecimento de transporte eletrônico, nf-e, ct-e

entry.title: SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management Localization: Innovations in 2502
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community!

Welcome back to the first blog post for the year 2025! This time, we bring you the latest innovations and features in localization of SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502

Here we are talking about a comprehensive solution that handles subscription and usage-based models. It provides a standardized approach to recurring billing, invoicing, tax compliance, and automation.

With each release, SAP introduces localized country/region versions and new functions to assist customers with tax compliance, billing, invoicing, automation, and streamlined processes. This not only helps reduce compliance risks and costs but also improves the sustainability of tax operations.

For example, in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502, several countries/regions and functions were delivered in the product area of SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management for localization.

Additional functions, which were not part of the roadmap, have also been delivered:

One example, from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 innovations, is the electronic submission of convergent invoices for Golden Tax invoice in China.

Electronic submission of convergent invoices for Golden Tax invoice in China

This video shows you how to submit invoices from convergent invoicing electronically using the integration of the golden tax interface.

 

For more information, I invite you to visit the following pages: 

  • Road Map Explorer: Future innovations planned 
  • SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management, public edition – Online Help Documentation
  • What’s New: What's New Viewer - SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Blog Post: SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management Localization: Sustainable Compliance for Your Recurring Revenue Business

The purpose of this post is to show you how SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management for SAP S/4HANA localization is a game-changer for businesses that are looking to streamline their financial operations while ensuring compliance with local regulations. Stay tuned for the next update!

I hope you’ve found this blog post interesting. If so, I invite you to follow my profile and the tags SAP Document and Reporting Compliance and SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management. Also, feel free to leave a comment or a suggestion in the comment section.

Thank you for choosing SAP to run your business complaint with local tax authorities!

entry.title: Exploring SAP's Regional Collaboration Groups on Localization Topics
entry.content:
SAP offers several channels for communication with its customers and partners to ensure effective support, feedback, and collaboration.  

The primary resource for it is the SAP Support Portal, where customers and partners can access support, product documentation, knowledge base articles, and use various self-service options.  

For direct communication on localization topics there are dozens of digital regional collaboration groups, or workspaces, managed by Local Product Managers (LPMs) of SAP. Each group is a network of SAP users, industry experts, and technology professionals, where they share ideas, knowledge, and experiences related to the localization in a certain region and country. All groups are divided by their product and solution category: ERP, HCM and Payroll, and SAP Document and Reporting Compliance. 

Members of the groups will be able to find fresh and useful materials on localization in their region: FAQs, learning material, links to numerous relevant resources and interesting blog posts. Additionally, you can discover links to:

- Regulatory Change Manager (RCM)

 - Localization community of SAP

-  SAP's localization event calendar

One of the essential functionalities of the workspaces is the Forum. It is an online discussion platform where members can engage in conversations, ask questions, share information, and collaborate. It is remarkable that all groups are closed and require authentication for access.

To engage with SAP users and experts on topics related to localization and upcoming regulatory changes in your country, please find here the workspace that aligns with your interests.

Do you want to stay informed about the upcoming events? Check out the SAP's localization event calendar.

Feel free to ask any questions about the local collaboration groups you may have in the comments section below.

entry.title: Local Roots, Global Impact: How Grupo Nabeiro Navigated the Business Landscape in Angola
entry.content:
As we continue our journey through a dynamic and ever-changing global landscape, we're thrilled to bring you the next captivating story in our ongoing blog series, featuring remarkable tales of innovation and resilience. 
In this exciting installment, we spotlight the incredible journey of Grupo Nabeiro, a Portugal-based coffee powerhouse, and their impressive strides in the complex market of Angola. 

Grupo Nabeiro: A Journey of Aromatic Transformation and Resilient Growth

In the aromatic and culturally rich world of coffee, stories of growth, triumph, and innovation abound. Among these, the journey of Portugal-based Grupo Nabeiro stands out as a remarkable tale of perseverance and transformation. Celebrating over 61 years of operation, the company, founded by Manuel Rui Azinhais Nabeiro, affectionately known as Mr. Rui Nabeiro, has woven an inspiring narrative of overcoming challenges in one of the world's most complex yet rewarding markets – Angola.

Grupo Nabeiro’s journey, which began in 1961 with a small coffee startup in Campo Maior, Portugal, has now expanded its footprints across eight countries. This transformation from a local coffee roaster to an international powerhouse is nothing short of extraordinary. Mr. Nabeiro’s vision was not simply about business but about touching lives and empowering communities through the universal language of coffee.
